## Summary

I wrote tests for the UI/UX finding (every row's trigger has the identical `aria-label 'Actions'`) in **`src/app/render-browse.test.ts`** (the only test file pinning the trigger's `aria-label`):

**Updated the existing structural test** (line 725) to assert the new desired value `'Actions for a.txt'` (was `'Actions'`), with an updated title and explanatory comment pointing to the new suite.

**Added a new focused `describe` block** ("trigger accessible name — aria-label includes the entry it acts on") with 9 independent tests:
- Includes the **file** name in the label
- Includes the **folder** name in the label
- Gives every row a **distinct** label (the core of the fix — a `Set` uniqueness check)
- Keeps the `"Actions for "` prefix (role still announced)
- Includes **only the name, not the full path**
- Interpolates the name **verbatim** (spaces/parens not mangled)
- Regression guard: visible `⋮` glyph unchanged
- Regression guard: `type`/`aria-haspopup`/`aria-expanded` unchanged
- Re-applies the per-row label after a re-render

**Verification:**
- ✅ TypeScript compiles with no errors
- ✅ Tests run cleanly (no runtime/compile errors)
- ✅ prettier + oxlint pass
- ✅ Math reconciles exactly: 20 pre-existing failures (unrelated: focus mgmt, `role=menu`, arrow keys, viewport clamping) + my 7 expected aria-label failures + 3 new passing regression guards; no collateral breakage

The 7 aria-label tests fail until the one-line source fix (`'Actions for ' + entry.name`) is applied, then turn green — correct TDD state for the write-tests step.
